channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: BCE terminates a few employees for repeatedly falsifying office attendance
item.description: BCE Inc., the parent company of Bell Canada, has terminated a small number of employees after discovering that they intentionally falsified their workplace attendance. The internal review revealed a p...

item.title: Geopolitical tensions wipe nearly R350bn off PIC assets amid market turmoil
item.description: The Public Investment Corporation (PIC), which manages funds including those of the Government Employee Pension Fund (GEPF), has reported a significant decline of প্রায় R350 billion in assets under ma...

item.title: Father Seeks Justice After Son Fatally Shot in Parow Valley
item.description: The Sampson family is grappling with grief following the tragic murder of their 24-year-old son, Egan Sampson, in Parow Valley earlier this year. Egan was shot multiple times in Denton Street, Ravensm...

item.title: RSU Law Professor Proposes Amendment to Land Use Act to Ease Land Ownership
item.description: Prof Grace Ogbonda Akolokwu, wey be Professor of Property and Human Rights Law for Rivers State University, don yan say dem suppose remove the consent provisions wey dey Sections 21, 22 and 28 of the ...

item.title: Federal budget unlikely to resolve underlying housing supply issues
item.description: The upcoming federal budget has sparked extensive debate and pre-release speculation, particularly around proposed changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax discount. These tax incentives ...

item.title: Four Highway Patrol Group officers investigated over alleged extortion of TNVS driver
item.description: Four police officers from the Philippine National Police’s Highway Patrol Group (HPG) are currently under investigation following an extortion complaint filed by a transport network vehicle service (T...

item.title: Traditional Costumes and Rituals on Italian Islands Captured in Photography
item.description: British photographer Alice Tomlinson spent two years documenting the traditional costumes worn by residents of the Venetian Lagoon, Sicily, and Sardinia during local celebrations. Her project, titled ...

item.title: Cantada acknowledges nerves affected her performance in UAAP Finals
item.description: During the UAAP Season 88 Finals, National University volleyball player Sam Cantada admitted that she felt overwhelmed by the pressure of the championship matches. Speaking to the media, Cantada share...

item.title: Labour faces criticism for including Net Zero targets in pothole funding scheme
item.description: Labour has been criticised for its new pothole funding scheme, with accusations that the party has sought to favour councils under its control by including Net Zero targets in the scoring system. The ...

item.title: Vita Buivid's Photographic Exploration of War Through Family Memories
item.description: Vita Buivid, an artist originally from Dnipro and currently based in Amsterdam, created the photo series 'How I Spend My Summer,' where she combines idyllic family photographs with images of military ...

item.title: Anthropic partners with SpaceX for expanded computing capacity to support AI
item.description: Anthropic, a prominent artificial intelligence company, has entered into an agreement with Elon Musk's SpaceX to enhance its computing capabilities and manage increasing demand for its Claude AI softw...

item.title: Frankie Valli’s son reportedly breaches restraining order, family matter resolved
item.description: Over the weekend, police attended the Los Angeles home of 92-year-old singer Frankie Valli following reports of a domestic dispute involving his estranged son, Francesco Valli Jr., 38. Authorities wer...

item.title: Health authorities trace flight contacts after isolated hantavirus case linked to cruise ship cluster
item.description: Global health authorities, including the World Health Organization (WHO) and the Africa CDC, are monitoring passengers and crew from an Airlink flight that travelled from St Helena to Johannesburg on ...

item.title: Ethiopia and Sudan Accuse Each Other Over Drone Strikes Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Ethiopia and Sudan don start serious blame game over recent drone attacks. Sudan claim say drones wey hit Khartoum airport and other military sites dey launched from Ethiopia, even say UAE dey involve...

item.title: Vasole Shoe Inserts Offer Effective Arch Support and Pain Relief at a Discounted Price
item.description: Many people struggle with foot, knee, and back pain due to inadequate footwear or long hours standing. Vasole shoe inserts, available on Amazon, are gaining attention for providing strong arch support...

item.title: DOH Confirms No Hantavirus Cases Among Filipino Crew in Quarantined Cruise Ship
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) confirmed that none of the 38 Filipino crew members on a cruise ship quarantined in Cabo Verde have shown symptoms of hantavirus. According to DOH spokesperson Albert Do...

item.title: Final Fragment of 17th-Century Flemish Tapestry Recovered by Spanish Police
item.description: Spanish authorities have recovered the last missing piece of a 17th-century Flemish tapestry, titled 'Apotheosis of the Arts,' which had been stolen 42 years ago. The large tapestry, measuring 4 by 6....

item.title: Tanzanian Universities Introduce Online Course to Help Students Report GBV and Access Health Services
item.description: University students in Tanzania are now gaining more knowledge and confidence to handle gender-based violence (GBV) thanks to the Our Rights, Our Lives, Our Future (O3 Plus) project. The initiative pr...

item.title: Nestoil Rejects Alleged Debt Claims, Says Dividend Suspension Linked to CBN Directive
item.description: Nestoil Limited don clear say all those stories wey dey talk say some Nigerian banks stop to pay dividends because dem dey owe Nestoil na lie. The company yan say na First Bank of Nigeria and their Ch...

item.title: Dutch Art Detective Recovers Six Stolen Paintings from Medemblik Town Hall
item.description: Dutch art detective Arthur Brand, renowned for his ability to recover stolen artworks, has successfully retrieved six paintings recently stolen from the Medemblik town hall in the Netherlands. This fo...
